title: rubus reflexus var. reflexus
intro: Rubus reflexus var. reflexus, commonly known as the reflexed thimbleberry, is a species of thimbleberry in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to New Zealand. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to act as an astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus reflexus var. reflexus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be consumed raw or used in a variety of herbal preparations, including salves and creams.
